what_it_does: This file is a ledger/model-card entry for Qwen/Qwen3.5-0.8B: it records architecture, size, context, tested vLLM performance, launch configuration, quirks, open questions, and links to related ledger entries.
observations: 0.8B vision-language model with hybrid Mamba-attention, BF16 weights, 262k native context, but only 4K tested.
Status is tested but not currently deployed; positioned as the high-concurrency/low-quality counterpart to the 4B AWQ variant.
Cold load time is 150.8s due to torch.compile and Mamba kernel JIT.
Three-prompt batch produced 91 tokens in 8.61s (10.6 tok/s aggregate) and a single-batch peak of 89 tok/s.
KV cache at 4K context is reported as 649,774 tokens, implying 158x max concurrency at 4K per request.
Suggested deployment swap uses the same chat template, tool-call parser, and prefix-caching config as the 4B model.
Vision tower carries VRAM cost even when no vision input is used.
Open items include a production concurrency sweep, tool/structured-output reliability tests, and identifying workloads that prefer this model over the 4B.
standouts: The 158x concurrency-at-4K claim based on tiny 1.7GB weights and large KV cache headroom.
It is tested but not deployed; the 4B AWQ took the production slot.
Explicitly positioned as a prototyping/research artifact rather than a default production chat model.
conventions: Uses ledger frontmatter (type, id, filename, status, runtime, dates, tags).
Uses wiki-style navigation links and an index hub link.
Uses Markdown tables for at-a-glance specs and performance metrics.
Includes a code block for the launch/swap command.
Ends with an 'Open at this entry' section and 'Connects to' backlinks.
concerns: The 158x concurrency figure is derived from KV cache capacity, not validated by a production-scale sweep.
Quality assessment is based on only three prompts and semantic spot-checks, not a benchmark.
Tool calling and structured output reliability are flagged as likely degraded but untested at this scale.
The '158x max concurrency' framing lacks a clear baseline or hardware context.
notes: A compact, tested model ledger entry that highlights extreme concurrency potential but leaves deployment decisions and quality validation open.
questions: What hardware/GPU was used for the reported load and generation times?
What baseline does the '158x max concurrency' claim compare against?
Which specific 'Tim-workloads' are candidates for this model versus the 4B AWQ?
Why did the 4B AWQ win the deployment slot over this much higher-concurrency option?
Is the chat template path ~/vllm-tests/chat_template_nothink.jinja shared or model-specific?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to document a tested model option, and the file does exactly that while also recording why it is not currently deployed.
apparent_themes: KV cache and concurrency budgeting as first-class evaluation.
Recording deployment decisions and their rationale in the ledger.
intention_signals: The entry is trying to capture a viable small-model alternative for mass-parallel, short-prompt work, make its swap-in configuration discoverable, and flag the tradeoffs and unknowns that would decide whether it ever deploys.
novelty: A sub-1B vision-language model with hybrid Mamba-attention is unusual as a high-concurrency chat candidate, and the ledger explicitly quantifies its KV-cache headroom as a 158x concurrency ceiling.
contribution: It contributes a tested performance snapshot, a concurrency-capacity estimate, a ready-to-use swap-in command, and a structured list of open questions for a small-model deployment option.
summary_for_embedding: Ledger entry for the tested-but-not-deployed Qwen3.5-0.8B BF16 chat model, documenting its tiny footprint, hybrid Mamba-attention architecture, 4K tested performance, 158x concurrency ceiling estimate, and open questions.
intention_for_embedding: This file was created to record a high-throughput, low-quality chat model alternative and the conditions under which it might replace the deployed 4B AWQ default.
